name: Baby Garden Snail
description: The baby Garden Snail keeps the same peaceful pace as the adult, but with less durability and even less ability to impose itself on a fight. It is slow, docile, and very easy to circle.
behavior: It moves without urgency and depends almost entirely on its shell and on how little most creatures want to bother with it.
interactionWithPlayer: It rarely creates real pressure on its own. If the player chooses to fight it, the encounter is more about time than immediate danger.
interactionWithCreatures: It shares the calm presence of the adult snail and spends far more time coexisting than dominating or fleeing.
strategy: There are not many tricks here: stay calm, punish the slow pace, and do not turn an easy fight into a long one by overextending for no reason.
